heading: Reserve Officer Training Corps units: limitation on number of Reserves assigned
label: 12321 Reserve Officer Training Corps units: limitation on number of Reserves assigned
text: The number of members of the reserve components serving on active duty or full-time National Guard duty for the purpose of organizing, administering, recruiting, instructing, or training the reserve components who are assigned to duty with a unit of the Reserve Officer Training Corps program may not exceed 275.
notes: Amendments1994— renumbered as this section and substituted “Reserve Officer Training Corps units: limitation on number of Reserves assigned” for “Limitation on duty with Reserve Officer Training Corps units” as section catchline.Pub. L. 103–337section 690 of this title1993— substituted “may not exceed 275” for “may not exceed 200”.Pub. L. 103–1601992— substituted “The number of members of the reserve components” for “A member of a reserve component”, “who are assigned” for “may not be assigned”, and “may not exceed 200.” for period at end.Pub. L. 102–4841991— substituted “Corps” for “Corp” in section catchline.Pub. L. 102–190, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–25, § 704(a)(3)(B)section 687 of this title, made technical correction to directory language of , which enacted this section.Pub. L. 102–25, § 704(a)(3)(A)Pub. L. 101–510, § 559(a)(1)

Waiver of Prohibition on Certain Reserve Service With ROTC Program, , , as amended by , , , permitted the Secretary of the military department concerned to waive the prohibition in this section in the case of a member of a reserve component of the Armed Forces who is serving with the Reserve Officer Training Corps program on , if the removal of the member from that assignment would cause a financial hardship for that member.Pub.
